page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Pride 2026: Parade, Party & Inclusive Cruises

data.meta_description: Book your Marbella yacht charter Pride 2026 with parade views, inclusive cruises from Puerto Banús. Prices from €749/2h, skipper & drinks included.
data.summary: Marbella yacht charter Pride 2026 offers exclusive parade views and inclusive cruises from Puerto Banús. Prices start at €749/2h with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Pride 2026 parade route passes Marbella Marina and Puerto Banús coastline.
Charters from Puerto Banús start at €749/2h (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39).
All boats include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance.
Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) available from €4,719/4h with free jet ski.
Book via WhatsApp for custom itineraries and special Pride packages.
Max capacity 12 pax per boat; ideal for groups up to 12.
Departures from Puerto Banús, just minutes from the parade action.

faq.q: What is the best boat for Pride 2026 in Marbella?
faq.a: For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) offers space and a free jet ski. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are excellent value from €749/2h.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Pride 2026?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom packages. We recommend booking early as Pride dates fill quickly. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

faq.q: Can we watch the Pride parade from the boat?
faq.a: Yes. The parade route runs along the Marbella coastline and Puerto Banús. Anchoring near the marina gives you front-row views with privacy and space to celebrate.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%).
No age restrictions for passengers. Children are welcome. The skipper ensures safety for all ages. Life jackets are available on board.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and decorations?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and Pride decorations. The boat has a fridge and table space. We provide snacks and drinks as standard.

faq.q: How long is a typical Pride charter?
faq.a: Most groups book 4-hour or 8-hour charters. The 2-hour option is available for Tier A boats (€749) if you want a quick parade view. Longer charters allow time to cruise to Cala del Faro or Estepona.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad on Pride day?
faq.a: We monitor forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe, we offer rescheduling or a full refund. The skipper makes the final call for safety.

data.body_html:
Marbella Pride 2026 promises a vibrant celebration of diversity, and there's no better way to experience it than from the deck of a private yacht. With the parade route skirting the Marbella coastline and Puerto Banús, a Marbella yacht charter Pride 2026 gives you front-row seats, unlimited drinks, and total privacy. Whether you're part of the parade or just want to soak up the atmosphere, our fleet departs from Puerto Banús and offers inclusive packages starting at €749 for two hours.

Why Choose a Yacht for Pride 2026?

Watching from a crowded beach bar is fun, but a yacht charter elevates the experience. You get uninterrupted views of the parade along the Golden Mile and Puerto Banús, your own sound system, and a dedicated crew. No queues for drinks, no jostling for space — just you, your guests, and the Mediterranean. Plus, you can cruise to quieter spots like Cala del Faro or Cabopino after the parade.

Our Fleet for Pride 2026

We offer over 15 boats from Puerto Banús, all with licensed skippers and inclusive pricing. For Pride 2026, the most popular choices are:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749/2h, €2,299/8h. Ideal for intimate groups.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – Same pricing, slightly larger capacity.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – From €4,719/4h, includes a free jet ski. Perfect for a party crowd.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – Spacious flybridge for sunbathing and views.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) – Ultra-luxury option for a flagship experience.

For other boats, pricing is available via WhatsApp. All charters include skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.

Pride Parade Route & Best Viewing Spots

The Marbella Pride parade typically starts at the Alameda Park, heads down the Golden Mile, and passes Puerto Banús. Anchoring just outside the marina gives you a perfect vantage point. From the boat, you'll see the floats, music stages, and crowds without the noise. After the parade, cruise to Cala Cortés or Estepona for a sunset swim.

Inclusive Cruises: What You Get

Every charter is fully inclusive. No hidden fees. Your package includes:

  • Licensed English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

You can bring your own food, decorations, and music. The boat has a Bluetooth speaker and fridge.

How to Book Your Pride 2026 Charter

Booking is simple. Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and budget. We'll recommend the best boat from our fleet. Pride 2026 dates are expected to be in late June; early booking is advised as demand is high. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly online. For larger yachts, we'll confirm availability within hours.

Check our main boat rental Marbella page for full fleet details and availability.

Tips for a Perfect Pride Day on the Water

  • Arrive 15 minutes early at Puerto Banús for boarding.
  • Bring sunscreen, hats, and sunglasses — the sun is strong.
  • Decorate your boat with Pride flags or balloons (we can help arrange).
  • Plan to anchor near the marina by 2pm for the best parade views.
  • Consider an 8-hour charter to combine parade watching with a cruise to Sotogrande.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best boat for Pride 2026 in Marbella?

For groups up to 12, the Mangusta 80 (24m) offers space and a free jet ski. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are excellent value from €749/2h.

How do I book a yacht for Pride 2026?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom packages. We recommend booking early as Pride dates fill quickly. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.

Can we watch the Pride parade from the boat?

Yes. The parade route runs along the Marbella coastline and Puerto Banús. Anchoring near the marina gives you front-row views with privacy and space to celebrate.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%).

Are there any age restrictions?

No age restrictions for passengers. Children are welcome. The skipper ensures safety for all ages. Life jackets are available on board.

Can we bring our own food and decorations?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and Pride decorations. The boat has a fridge and table space. We provide snacks and drinks as standard.

How long is a typical Pride charter?

Most groups book 4-hour or 8-hour charters. The 2-hour option is available for Tier A boats (€749) if you want a quick parade view. Longer charters allow time to cruise to Cala del Faro or Estepona.

What if the weather is bad on Pride day?

We monitor forecasts closely. If conditions are unsafe, we offer rescheduling or a full refund. The skipper makes the final call for safety.

Ready to celebrate Pride 2026 in style? Browse our full fleet on the Marbella yacht charter homepage and book your inclusive cruise today.
